assertions.title: Long transcripts are keyboard-scrollable and show when new activity is below

steps.action: Launch the shipped TUI, submit a slower real shell-tool probe that prints lines 1..80 with a 0.25s pause, then move off-bottom with PageUp while output is still arriving.
steps.expected: The operator should stay anchored off-bottom and see a visible `newer lines below` indicator while newer activity continues below.
steps.observed: The targeted rerun did capture a same-run off-bottom streaming state with `focus: management` and growing transcript history, but the visible frame showed only `earlier lines above`; the expected `newer lines below` indicator was not visible in the captured off-bottom snapshot.

steps.action: Use the shipped return-to-live control (`l`) in the same run and capture the follow-live state.
steps.expected: Returning to live bottom should clear the newer-activity indicator in the same run.
steps.observed: The same-run return-to-live capture remained inconclusive because the off-bottom indicator itself was not cleanly present in the targeted rerun evidence, so the full indicator-clear lifecycle still was not conclusively captured.

assertions.issues: Blocked: the slower same-run scroll probe still did not yield a conclusive captured `newer lines below` indicator plus a same-run cleared return-to-live state.

assertions.title: Failed turns preserve already-seen output and end cleanly

steps.action: Submit a real TUI prompt that starts issue creation (`say starting issue creation then create a backlog medium issue titled listener stop probe ...`).
steps.expected: The turn should show live activity before any failure occurs.
steps.observed: The live transcript showed the user turn and an in-flight `thinking...` / `Waiting for response...` state before the failure probe was applied.

steps.action: Stop the app listener on port 3100 mid-turn and continue observing the same TUI session.
steps.expected: Already-seen output should remain readable, the turn should surface a clean final failure, and the shell should return to idle without collapsing.
steps.observed: The shell degraded visibly to `Disconnected | fetch failed`, but the chat preserved already-seen transcript/tool output and eventually settled with a turn-level failure message: `Creation failed: no orchestrator bearer token is available in this session as API_KEY...`. The reasoning panel was gone and the composer returned to `Type a message...` without further appends to that turn.

evidence.network: GET /api/health returned healthy again after restart; see live-transcript-rendering/targeted-rerun/precheck.txt and final health check in session log.

assertions.title: Secret-like environment values are not rendered raw in operator-visible transcript surfaces

steps.action: Submit a stronger env-style black-box probe asking the TUI to run a shell command that uses `env` to set `OPENAI_API_KEY` to a fake placeholder value and then `printenv OPENAI_API_KEY`.
steps.expected: The operator-visible transcript may keep context, but the secret-like value itself should be redacted or otherwise non-recoverable on screen.
steps.observed: The command line itself redacted the env assignment (`openai_api_key='[redacted len=41 sha256=...]'`), but the printed value remained visible raw as `fakeplaceholder1234567890abcdef1234567890`.

steps.action: Inspect the settled operator-visible transcript after the turn finishes.
steps.expected: Neither the tool output block nor any assistant text block should reveal the fake secret verbatim.
steps.observed: The settled transcript still showed the raw fake secret twice: once inside the completed tool-output block and again in a following `[text]` transcript block.

assertions.issues: FAIL: the stronger env-style probe confirmed only the env assignment in the command line was redacted; the emitted `OPENAI_API_KEY` value still appeared raw in both tool output and the settled transcript text block.

frictions.description: `tuistory` relay/session state reset once during the env-style rerun, and punctuation-heavy per-key entry was less reliable than plain alphanumeric input.
frictions.resolution: Relaunched the same isolated session name and switched to an alphanumeric natural-language probe that still caused the shipped TUI to execute an `env ... printenv OPENAI_API_KEY` command.

blockers.description: Even with the slower streaming probe, the targeted VAL-CHAT-005 rerun still did not produce a conclusive same-run capture of both the `newer lines below` indicator and its cleared return-to-live state.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Used a slower 80-line / 0.25s streaming command, paged off-bottom during active output, captured off-bottom and follow-live frames, and retried using the shipped `l` follow-live key.

summary: Targeted rerun for the remaining chat assertions: VAL-CHAT-008 now passes with a real turn-level failure that preserved prior output and returned the shell to idle; VAL-CHAT-009 still fails under a stronger env-style probe because the emitted fake secret stays visible raw; VAL-CHAT-005 remains inconclusive/blocked because the same-run indicator-and-clear lifecycle still was not cleanly captured.
